summary: Matched the previously-asm item file `it_2F2B` as C, replacing `asm/melee/it/items/it_2F2B.s` with `src/melee/it/items/it_2F2B.c` and updating `obj_files.mk` to build the C object. The matched file covers 412 bytes at 100%, including `it_802F2BFC`, `it_802F2CE0`, three small item-state callbacks, and the `it_803F9480` item state table. The work also tightened related prototypes and types around Yoshi shield/item ownership, including `ftYs_Shield_8012CC6C` returning `Fighter_Part`, `ftLib_80086630` taking a `Fighter_Part`, item/fighter gobj-specific parameters, and `Fighter::x1A60` as `Item_GObj*`. No review comments were present in the slice.
what_changed: Deleted the 139-line assembly implementation `asm/melee/it/items/it_2F2B.s` and added a 72-line C implementation in `src/melee/it/items/it_2F2B.c`.
Implemented `it_803F9480` as a one-entry `ItemStateTable` with state id `-1` and callbacks `it_802F2CCC`, `it_802F2CD4`, and `it_802F2CD8`.

Implemented `it_802F2BFC(Item_GObj* gobj, ftYs_GObj* owner_gobj)` to bind an item to Yoshi shield ownership: set `atk_victim` and `grab_victim`, call `it_8026C220`, clear `xDD0_flag.bits.b1`, set the item state table, attach the item to the owner shield part, and run item setup helpers.

Implemented `it_802F2CE0(Item_GObj* gobj, bool has_destroy_type)` to detach/cleanup the item: call `it_80275070`, compute item position from the owner's shield joint via `ftLib_80086630` and `lb_8000B1CC`, call release/update helpers, optionally clear `destroy_type`, null `atk_victim` and `grab_victim`, then call `it_8027CE44` and `Item_8026A8EC`.
Changed `ftYs_Shield_8012CC6C` from a void helper to `Fighter_Part ftYs_Shield_8012CC6C(ftYs_GObj* gobj)` and returned the result of `ftParts_8007500C(GET_FIGHTER(gobj), 52)`.
Changed `ftLib_80086630` from taking a raw `s32 i` to taking `Fighter_Part part`, and used that enum-like part index to access `fp->parts[part].joint`.
Updated item APIs to use more specific gobj types: `it_8027B4A4(Fighter_GObj*, Item_GObj*)` and `Item_8026A8EC(Item_GObj*)`.
Expanded `it_2725.h` with address-commented prototypes needed by the new C file, including `it_80273408`, `it_80274C88`, `it_80274ECC`, `it_80274F48`, `it_80275070`, `it_8027B378`, and `it_8027CE44`.
Removed automatic inclusion of `ftCommon/forward.h` from `tools/functions.sh` header generation.
smart_moves: The PR did not merely force a local match; it corrected upstream prototypes and return types so the C reads naturally and preserves meaningful dataflow, especially the returned `Fighter_Part` from `ftYs_Shield_8012CC6C`.

Using typed aliases such as `Item_GObj*`, `Fighter_GObj*`, `ftYs_GObj*`, and `Fighter_Part` made the item-owner relationship explicit and helped express the assembly calls without generic `HSD_GObj*` or raw integer placeholders.

The implementation reuses existing item and fighter helpers instead of open-coding behavior: `it_80274F48`, `it_80275070`, `ftLib_80086630`, `lb_8000B1CC`, `it_8027B4A4`, `it_8027B378`, and item destruction helpers.
The assembly table `it_803F9480` was translated into a typed `ItemStateTable` initializer rather than left as raw data, improving future item-state decomp readability.
The small callback functions were represented directly as `return false` and an empty function body, matching the trivial assembly while keeping callback signatures consistent.
The cleanup function keeps the optional `destroy_type` write guarded by the boolean argument, matching the conditional assembly behavior instead of flattening it.
decomp_lessons: When an assembly routine passes the result of a helper into another function, revisit the helper's return type; here `ftYs_Shield_8012CC6C` needed to return `Fighter_Part` rather than `void`.
Raw `s32` part indices should become `Fighter_Part` when they index `Fighter::parts`; this improves correctness and makes call chains involving fighter joints easier to reason about.
Item files that interact with owners often require more precise gobj typing across neighboring headers before the item C will be clean and reusable.
Translating an item state table to `ItemStateTable` with typed callbacks is preferable to keeping raw `.4byte` table data once callback signatures are known.

Field names such as `atk_victim`, `grab_victim`, `xBC_itemStateContainer`, `xC4_article_data`, `xDD0_flag.bits.b1`, `destroy_type`, and `pos` can replace offset-heavy assembly patterns when the item struct is sufficiently understood.
Header cleanup can be part of matching work: add only the forward declarations/includes needed by the matched file and avoid generated headers pulling unrelated forward headers by default.
The evidence shows this PR partially resolved issue #1059, but the slice does not include that issue context, so the exact broader task is not documented here.
naming_conventions: Matched functions kept address-based names: `it_802F2BFC`, `it_802F2CCC`, `it_802F2CD4`, `it_802F2CD8`, and `it_802F2CE0`.
The item state table global kept its address-based symbol name `it_803F9480`.
The new header uses an address-derived include guard `GALE01_2F2BFC`.
Header prototypes in `it_2725.h` use address comments such as `/* 274F48 */` and named parameters where known.
More specific typedef-style object names are preferred over generic `HSD_GObj*` when known: `Item_GObj*`, `Fighter_GObj*`, and `ftYs_GObj*`.
Boolean state callbacks use `bool` and return `false` instead of raw integer `0` in C.
The cleanup argument was named semantically as `has_destroy_type`, reflecting the conditional clearing of `item->destroy_type`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed the entire assembly file after achieving a reported 100.00% match for the 412-byte C file.
Converted the assembly `.data` table with `-1` and three callback pointers into a one-element `ItemStateTable` initializer.
Matched bit manipulation of byte `0xDD0` as `item->xDD0_flag.bits.b1 = 0`.
Matched pointer stores at item offsets corresponding to owner/victim links as `item->atk_victim = owner_gobj` and `item->grab_victim = owner_gobj`.
Matched nested article/model descriptor loads for bone attach id as `item->xC4_article_data->x10_modelDesc->x8_bone_attach_id`.
Preserved call order from assembly in setup and cleanup functions, which is often critical for exact matching and behavior.
Represented the `li r3, 0; blr` callbacks as `return false` and the pure `blr` callback as an empty void function.
Replaced the assembly zero constant `it_804DDA08` used for `it_8027B378` with literal `0` in C.
Used `NULL` for zero pointer arguments and cleared item owner/victim pointers with `NULL` during cleanup.
follow_up_queries: Find other item files that call `ftYs_Shield_8012CC6C` and may benefit from the corrected `Fighter_Part` return type.
Search for raw `s32` parameters indexing `Fighter::parts` that should be converted to `Fighter_Part`.
Search for remaining `HSD_GObj*` item APIs that can be narrowed to `Item_GObj*` or `Fighter_GObj*` based on call sites.
Investigate issue #1059 to determine which remaining files or bytes were part of the same partial resolution.
Check whether generated headers still include only necessary forward declarations after removing `ftCommon/forward.h` from `tools/functions.sh`.
